summary: Good soundboard capture of a lively ’88 show featuring a psychedelic Other One and standout Brent organ work.
review: The recording is a clean digital SBD source that delivers the band’s 1988 energy without major drop‑outs.  Fans rave about the wild, psych‑delirious The Other One and the soaring organ on Rooster, while a few songs (Box of Rain, Cumberland Blues) are called out as weaker moments.  Overall the second set flows nicely through spacey jams into an excellent Knockin’ finale.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance and note a solid soundboard recording; occasional criticisms are outweighed by strong positive comments, yielding an overall rating just above four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Digital PCM SBD master; clear and balanced with no major drop‑outs, though some listeners comment on unusual spatial/phasing effects that add character.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set opens with solid classics (Box of Rain, Hell in a Bucket, Touch of Grey).  The second set builds momentum with Louie/Truckin' into an intense jam sequence—Drums → Space—and peaks with the soaring Other One and Rooster before closing on a high‑energy Knockin'.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Jerry’s guitar work is highlighted on the wild, psychedelic Other One; several reviewers note his tone and improvisational fire.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass gets mixed feedback—some feel it could have been more prominent, while others appreciate its steady foundation.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s vocals are praised on ‘Throwing’ (Stones), adding energy to the set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s organ is a major highlight, especially on Rooster and during the space‑filled jams; fans specifically call out his playing as superb.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming drives the transition into an intense Space segment and is noted for its tight, propulsive feel.
